title: Verb: hunu
content:
Lemma: hunu (to be / to happen / to occur)
Semantic: COPULA_EVENT
Variants: bhayo, vayo, bho, vo, bhayeko, vayeko, bhaye, vaye, bhayena, vayena, huncha, hunchha, hunxa, hunchhan, hola, hoina, hos, hunu, huney, hudaina
Common typos: vo, vayo, xha, hunxa, bhayeyo, vayeyo, bhaio, vaio, thio, thi
Negation: bhayena, vayena, bhoina, voina, hudaina, hunna, chhaina, xaina
Signals completion: True
Notes: cha/chha/xa/xha are orthographic variants of the same present copula; vo/vayo = bhayo in colloquial Romanization; thiyo/thyo = past copula 'was'
Examples:
- parser: cha/chha/xa/xha are orthographic variants of the same present copula; vo/vayo = bhayo in colloquial Romanization; thiyo/thyo = past copula 'was'

metadata.parser_notes: cha/chha/xa/xha are orthographic variants of the same present copula; vo/vayo = bhayo in colloquial Romanization; thiyo/thyo = past copula 'was'

title: Verb: chanu
content:
Lemma: chanu (to be (present / existent))
Semantic: EXISTENCE_COPULA
Variants: cha, chha, xa, xha, chhan, xhan, chhau, xau, chhu, xhu, chhe, che, rahechha, rahecha, raicha, raixa, raichhan, raixhan, thiyo, thyo
Common typos: xa, xha, xhu, raixa, xhan, chaina, thio, tiyo
Negation: chhaina, xaina, chaina, thiyena, rahechha'ina
Signals completion: False
Notes: cha/chha/xa/xha are same morpheme; xhu/chhu = I am/have; raicha = evidential 'it turns out to be'; critical for distinguishing 'X cha' (exists) vs 'X bhayo' (happened)
Examples:
- parser: cha/chha/xa/xha are same morpheme; xhu/chhu = I am/have; raicha = evidential 'it turns out to be'; critical for distinguishing 'X cha' (exists) vs 'X bhayo' (happened)

metadata.parser_notes: cha/chha/xa/xha are same morpheme; xhu/chhu = I am/have; raicha = evidential 'it turns out to be'; critical for distinguishing 'X cha' (exists) vs 'X bhayo' (happened)

title: Verb: bhayeko
content:
Lemma: bhayeko (has happened / was done (perfect aspect marker))
Semantic: PERFECT_ASPECT
Variants: bhayeko, vayeko, bhyeko, vyeko, bhaeko, vaeko, bhaeko cha, bhayeko cha, vayeko cha, bhaisakeko, vaisakeko, garisakeko, bhaichhaeko, bhaisako, vaisako, bhai raheko, vai raheko, gareko
Common typos: bhaeko, vaeko, bhyeko, vyeko, bhayeko cha nai, vayeko xa nai, baisako, vaisako
Negation: bhayeko chhaina, vayeko xaina, bhayena, vayena, nabhayeko
Signals completion: True
Notes: bhayeko = completive/perfect marker; stacks on action verbs 'gareko bhayeko cha' = it has been done; vayeko is colloquial romanization common in Terai and younger writers
Examples:
- parser: bhayeko = completive/perfect marker; stacks on action verbs 'gareko bhayeko cha' = it has been done; vayeko is colloquial romanization common in Terai and younger writers

metadata.parser_notes: bhayeko = completive/perfect marker; stacks on action verbs 'gareko bhayeko cha' = it has been done; vayeko is colloquial romanization common in Terai and younger writers

title: Verb: rahanu
content:
Lemma: rahanu (to remain / to stay / to continue being)
Semantic: CONTINUITY_STATE
Variants: raheko, rahyo, rahane, rah, raha, rahan, rahirako, rahibhako, rahichhu, rahinchha, rahichhan, rahiraheko, baseko, base, basi, rahisakeko, rahirahanchha, rahena, basena
Common typos: rahirheko, rahi rako, rahirako cha, basiraheko, rahieko
Negation: rahena, raheko chhaina, basena, baseko chhaina, rahina sakyo
Signals completion: False
Notes: rahi+verb = continuous aspect marker; 'rahi raheko' = was staying continuously; baseko often substitutes raheko in colloquial speech for people/items remaining at a place
Examples:
- parser: rahi+verb = continuous aspect marker; 'rahi raheko' = was staying continuously; baseko often substitutes raheko in colloquial speech for people/items remaining at a place

metadata.parser_notes: rahi+verb = continuous aspect marker; 'rahi raheko' = was staying continuously; baseko often substitutes raheko in colloquial speech for people/items remaining at a place

title: Verb: saknu
content:
Lemma: saknu (to be able to / to complete / to finish)
Semantic: ABILITY_COMPLETION
Variants: sakyo, sakeko, sakine, sakdaina, sakdina, sakincha, sakunchha, sakchhu, sakchha, sakiyo, sakyena, sakena, sakisakyo, garisakyo, sakchhina, sakdainau, sak
Common typos: sakiyo, sakyena, sakdina, sakdainna, sakisakyo, sakecho
Negation: sakdaina, sakdina, sakena, sakyena, sakisakena, sakinna
Signals completion: True
Notes: sakyo = action completed successfully; sakdaina = cannot/unable; stacks as postverbal auxiliary: 'garnu + sakyo' = was able to do / finished doing; sakisakyo = has fully completed
Examples:
- parser: sakyo = action completed successfully; sakdaina = cannot/unable; stacks as postverbal auxiliary: 'garnu + sakyo' = was able to do / finished doing; sakisakyo = has fully completed

metadata.parser_notes: sakyo = action completed successfully; sakdaina = cannot/unable; stacks as postverbal auxiliary: 'garnu + sakyo' = was able to do / finished doing; sakisakyo = has fully completed

title: Verb: parnu
content:
Lemma: parnu (must / have to / to fall / obligation)
Semantic: OBLIGATION_NECESSITY
Variants: parcha, parchha, parxa, paryo, pariyo, pareko, parne, parna, pardaina, pardina, paryena, parena, parla, paro, par, parbhayo, pari, parisakyo
Common typos: parxa, parcha, parbhayo, pardina, paryena, pareko cha
Negation: pardaina, pardina, parena, paryena, parne chhaina
Signals completion: False
Notes: parcha = must/need to (present obligation); paryo = obligation was fulfilled or event occurred; 'tirna parcha' = must pay; 'paryo nai' = it happened anyway / unavoidably; stacks on infinitive to express obligation
Examples:
- parser: parcha = must/need to (present obligation); paryo = obligation was fulfilled or event occurred; 'tirna parcha' = must pay; 'paryo nai' = it happened anyway / unavoidably; stacks on infinitive to express obligation

title: Verb: thaha hunu
content:
Lemma: thaha hunu (to know / to be aware)
Semantic: KNOWLEDGE_AWARENESS
Variants: thaha cha, thaha chha, thaha xa, thaha thiyo, thaha thyo, thaha chhaina, thaha xaina, thaha bhayo, thaha vayo, thaha payeko, thaha chha malaai, thaha chhu, patta cha, patta lagyo, thaha lagyo, thaha nai chhaina, pataa chhaina
Common typos: taha cha, taha chhaina, thaha xa, thaha xaina, pata cha, pata lagyo
Negation: thaha chhaina, thaha xaina, thaha chaina, pataa chhaina, thaha nai chhaina
Signals completion: False
Notes: thaha cha = I know (lit. knowledge is); thaha bhayo = came to know / realized; patta lagyo = found out; critical for queries like 'malaai thaha chhaina kati baki cha' = I don't know how much is due
Examples:
- parser: thaha cha = I know (lit. knowledge is); thaha bhayo = came to know / realized; patta lagyo = found out; critical for queries like 'malaai thaha chhaina kati baki cha' = I don't know how much is due

title: Verb: chahanu
content:
Lemma: chahanu (to want / to need / to desire)
Semantic: DESIRE_INTENT
Variants: chahanchhu, chahanchha, chahanxhu, chahanxa, chahiyo, chahine, chahiyeko, chahinchha, chahinxa, chahdaina, chahdina, chahana, chaahinchha, chaahinxa, chahana parcha, chaheko, chaayo
Common typos: chahinxa, chahinchha, chaayo, chaahiyo, chaahdaina, chahina
Negation: chahdaina, chahdina, chahinna, chaahdaina, chahinchha'ina
Signals completion: False
Notes: chahiyo = was needed/wanted (impersonal); chahinchha = is needed; 'malaai 5000 chahiyo' = I need/needed 5000; distinguishes intentional purchase vs accidental receipt; strong intent signal for parser
Examples:
- parser: chahiyo = was needed/wanted (impersonal); chahinchha = is needed; 'malaai 5000 chahiyo' = I need/needed 5000; distinguishes intentional purchase vs accidental receipt; strong intent signal for parser

metadata.parser_notes: chahiyo = was needed/wanted (impersonal); chahinchha = is needed; 'malaai 5000 chahiyo' = I need/needed 5000; distinguishes intentional purchase vs accidental receipt; strong intent signal for parser

title: Verb: aunu
content:
Lemma: aunu (to come / to arrive / to know how to (skill))
Semantic: ARRIVAL_ABILITY
Variants: aayo, aauyo, aai, aaeko, aayeko, aaune, aaunchha, aaunchhu, aauna, aauna sakyo, aayena, aaudaina, aaudina, aaula, aauxa, aaisisakyo, aaisisakeko, aaisako
Common typos: aayeyo, aaeko, aaisakyo, aauxa, aaudina, aayna
Negation: aayena, aaudaina, aaudina, aauna sakena, aaisako chhaina
Signals completion: False
Notes: aayo = came/arrived; also means 'income received' in context: 'payment aayo' = payment came in; 'malaai aaucha' = I know how to (skill sense); dual role makes contextual disambiguation critical
Examples:
- parser: aayo = came/arrived; also means 'income received' in context: 'payment aayo' = payment came in; 'malaai aaucha' = I know how to (skill sense); dual role makes contextual disambiguation critical

metadata.parser_notes: aayo = came/arrived; also means 'income received' in context: 'payment aayo' = payment came in; 'malaai aaucha' = I know how to (skill sense); dual role makes contextual disambiguation critical

title: Verb: pugnu
content:
Lemma: pugnu (to reach / to be enough / to suffice)
Semantic: SUFFICIENCY_ARRIVAL
Variants: pugyo, pugiyo, pugeko, pugne, pugchha, pugxa, pugchhu, pugdaina, pugdina, pugena, pugla, pugos, pugisakyo, pugera, puger, puganechha, pugna, alikai pugdaina

Negation: pugdaina, pugdina, pugena, pugisakena, pugla jasto chhaina
Signals completion: True
Notes: pugyo = reached destination OR amount was sufficient; 'paisa pugdaina' = money is not enough; 'stock pugyo' = stock is sufficient; dual sense (physical arrival vs sufficiency) requires amount/context parsing
Examples:
- parser: pugyo = reached destination OR amount was sufficient; 'paisa pugdaina' = money is not enough; 'stock pugyo' = stock is sufficient; dual sense (physical arrival vs sufficiency) requires amount/context parsing

title: Verb: bujhnu
content:
Lemma: bujhnu (to understand / to comprehend / to receive (payment in some contexts))
Semantic: COMPREHENSION_RECEIPT
Variants: bujheko, bujhyo, bujhiyo, bujhine, bujhchhu, bujhchha, bujhdaina, bujhdina, bujhena, bujhisakyo, bujhaeko, bujhaiyo, bujhla, bujhnus, bujhai, bujiyo, bujhera

Negation: bujhdaina, bujhdina, bujhena, bujhisakena, bujheko chhaina
Signals completion: False
Notes: bujhyo = understood; also used in accounting context: 'paisa bujhaeko' = payment acknowledged/received (formal); 'bujhnus' = please understand/note — common in formal requests
Examples:
- parser: bujhyo = understood; also used in accounting context: 'paisa bujhaeko' = payment acknowledged/received (formal); 'bujhnus' = please understand/note — common in formal requests

metadata.parser_notes: bujhyo = understood; also used in accounting context: 'paisa bujhaeko' = payment acknowledged/received (formal); 'bujhnus' = please understand/note — common in formal requests

title: Verb: birsanu
content:
Lemma: birsanu (to forget / to omit accidentally)
Semantic: OMISSION_FORGET
Variants: birseko, birsyo, birsiye, birsine, birsichhu, birsichha, birsinchha, birsideko, birsidechha, birsiyeko, birseko cha, birse, birsiyo, birsisakyo, birsaeko, birseko nai
Common typos: birse ko, birseko cha, birsiyo, birsaeko, birsideko, biriseko
Negation: birseko chhaina, birsina, birsisakena, birseko hoina, nai birseko
Signals completion: False
Notes: birseko = forgot (critical for detecting missed entries: 'entry birseko raicha' = turns out the entry was forgotten); high relevance for error-correction in bookkeeping prompts
Examples:
- parser: birseko = forgot (critical for detecting missed entries: 'entry birseko raicha' = turns out the entry was forgotten); high relevance for error-correction in bookkeeping prompts

metadata.parser_notes: birseko = forgot (critical for detecting missed entries: 'entry birseko raicha' = turns out the entry was forgotten); high relevance for error-correction in bookkeeping prompts

title: Verb: samjhanu
content:

Signals completion: False
Notes: samjheko = recalled/remembered; samjhnus = please note/remember (used in instructions); 'malai samjhiyo' = it occurred to me — relevant for detecting follow-up corrections or reminders in conversation

title: Verb: sodhnu
content:
Lemma: sodhnu (to ask / to inquire / to check)
Semantic: INQUIRY_QUERY

Common typos: sodhiyo, sodhecho, sodiyo, sodhisakyo, sodheko cha, sodna
Negation: sodhena, sodhisakena, sodheko chhaina, nasodheko, sodhdaina
Signals completion: False
Notes: sodhyo = asked/inquired; 'rate sodheko' = inquired about rate; 'hisab sodhna' = to ask for the account; imperative sodhnus is used in polite follow-up requests; signals need for information retrieval in parser
Examples:

title: Verb: bhannu
content:
Lemma: bhannu (to say / to tell / to mean / to call something)
Semantic: SPEECH_LABEL
Variants: bhanneko, bhanyo, bhaniye, bhane, bhancha, bhanchha, bhanxa, bhanchhu, bhanxhu, bhaniyo, bhan, bhannu, bhannus, bhaney, bhaneko, bhandai, bhandaicha, bhanisakyo, bhanera
Common typos: bhaney, bhaneko cha, bhaniyo, bhanxha, bhandaicha, bhanera ko
Negation: bhanena, bhaneko chhaina, nabhaneko, nabhanyo, nabhannus
Signals completion: False
Notes: bhaneko / bhanera = quotative marker ('X bhaneko' = what is called X); critical for parsing named entities: 'Ram bhaneko manchhe = person named Ram'; 'bhandai' = while saying; 'bhanyo re' = reportedly said (hearsay evidential)
Examples:
- parser: bhaneko / bhanera = quotative marker ('X bhaneko' = what is called X); critical for parsing named entities: 'Ram bhaneko manchhe = person named Ram'; 'bhandai' = while saying; 'bhanyo re' = reportedly said (hearsay evidential)

title: Verb: sunnu
content:
Lemma: sunnu (to hear / to listen / to obey)
Semantic: AUDITORY_RECEIPT
Variants: suneko, sunyo, suniyo, sunne, sunchhu, sunchha, suninchha, sunideko, sunisakyo, sunera, suni, sun, sunnus, suniyeko, sundaina, sundina, sunena
Common typos: suniyo, sunecho, sunisakyo, suneko cha, sun na
Negation: sundaina, sundina, sunena, sunisakena, suneko chhaina
Signals completion: False
Notes: sunyo = heard; 'kura sunnus' = please listen/heed; in business context 'rate suneko' = heard the quoted rate; 'bhaneko sunnuhunchha' = reportedly heard; used to detect second-hand info in transaction descriptions
Examples:
- parser: sunyo = heard; 'kura sunnus' = please listen/heed; in business context 'rate suneko' = heard the quoted rate; 'bhaneko sunnuhunchha' = reportedly heard; used to detect second-hand info in transaction descriptions

title: Verb: dekhnu
content:

Semantic: VISUAL_CHECK
Variants: dekheko, dekhyo, dekhiyo, dekhne, dekhchhu, dekhchha, dekhinchha, dekhideko, dekhisakyo, dekhera, dekhi, dekh, dekhnus, dekhiyeko, dekhdaina, dekhdina, dekhena, dekhaideko

Signals completion: False
Notes: dekhyo = saw/noticed; dekhnus = please check/see; in data context 'hisab dekhnus' = please check the account; often used with 'herna' as synonym; 'dekhiyeko chhaina' = hasn't been seen/checked yet
Examples:
- parser: dekhyo = saw/noticed; dekhnus = please check/see; in data context 'hisab dekhnus' = please check the account; often used with 'herna' as synonym; 'dekhiyeko chhaina' = hasn't been seen/checked yet

title: Verb: khojnu
content:

Semantic: SEARCH_SEEK

title: Verb: paunu
content:
Lemma: paunu (to get / to receive / to find / to obtain)
Semantic: OBTAIN_RECEIVE
Variants: paayeko, paayo, payeko, payo, paaeko, paai, paine, painchha, painxa, paunchhu, paunchha, pauna, paiyeko, paiyo, payena, paudaina, paudina, paena, paaisakyo, pai
Common typos: payeko, paaisakyo, paudina, paiyo, paeko, paaiyo
Negation: payena, paudaina, paudina, paena, paiena, pauna sakena
Signals completion: True
Notes: paayo = received/got (key inbound receipt signal); 'payment paayo' = received payment; 'paaisakyo' = has completely received; 'paudaina' = can't get / not available; semantically overlaps with linu (take) but implies passive receipt rather than active taking
Examples:
- parser: paayo = received/got (key inbound receipt signal); 'payment paayo' = received payment; 'paaisakyo' = has completely received; 'paudaina' = can't get / not available; semantically overlaps with linu (take) but implies passive receipt rather than active taking

title: Verb: haalnu
content:
Lemma: haalnu (to put / to enter / to deposit / to add)
Semantic: INPUT_ENTRY
Variants: haaleko, haalyo, haaliyo, haalne, haalchhu, haalchha, haalinchha, haalideko, haalisakyo, haalera, haali, haal, haalnus, haaliyeko, haaldaina, haaldina, haalena, haalaeko, halideko
Common typos: haaliyo, haalecho, haalisakyo, haaleko cha, haali deko
Negation: haaldaina, haaldina, haalena, haalisakena, haaleko chhaina
Signals completion: True
Notes: haalyo = put in / entered / deposited; 'entry haaldinus' = please enter it; 'account ma paisa haalyo' = deposited money into account; 'system ma haaleko chhaina' = hasn't been entered in the system — key signal for missing data entry detection
Examples:
- parser: haalyo = put in / entered / deposited; 'entry haaldinus' = please enter it; 'account ma paisa haalyo' = deposited money into account; 'system ma haaleko chhaina' = hasn't been entered in the system — key signal for missing data entry detection

metadata.parser_notes: haalyo = put in / entered / deposited; 'entry haaldinus' = please enter it; 'account ma paisa haalyo' = deposited money into account; 'system ma haaleko chhaina' = hasn't been entered in the system — key signal for missing data entry detection
